_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: LOCAL batch: Raleigh NC city council batch-2 (patton, silver, forte) + NC district attorneys (merriweather/Mecklenburg, freeman/Wake). Evidence-only; social-media/questionnaire/endorsement protocol; consistent with natl-local-nc-2026-06-10-0515 precedents (unanimous FY27 tax-increase budget vote = econ q2 FALSE; Equality NC PAC endorsement = bm q4 FALSE; affirmative pro-police platform = pj q0 TRUE).

profile.education: BA, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill (2010)
profile.background: Customer service manager (e-commerce); former elementary school teacher; first elected 2022, re-elected 2024 (defeated Jennifer McCollum ~55-45)

megan-patton.notes_set: Raleigh City Council District B (nonpartisan; self-identified Democrat). First elected Nov 2022; re-elected Nov 2024; running unopposed for re-election Nov 3 2026 (primary canceled). Chairs Transportation & Transit Committee. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10: pro-choice (Vote Pro Choice endorsement 2022), Equality NC PAC-endorsed 2024, Moms Demand Action member who petitioned senators for gun legislation, voted for unanimous FY2027 tax-increase budget and prior FY2024 4-cent increase. On policing: opposes defunding but advocates shifting non-violent calls to unarmed responders (HEARTS/ART model) and strengthening the Police Advisory Board — no affirmative pro-police advocacy found, so public_justice q0 is null (high-bar rule). No evidence found on schools, religion, elections, state-overreach, or immigration.

0.note: Accepted the 2022 Vote Pro Choice endorsement (listed on her own campaign endorsements page) — single-issue pro-abortion-rights endorsement, contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Vote Pro Choice-endorsed candidate; documented pro-abortion-rights alignment, contrary to abolition advocacy.

0.note: No direct statement on marriage found.

2.note: No trans-policy statement or vote found in her name.

4.note: Accepted the 2024 Equality NC PAC endorsement — a single-issue LGBT-advocacy endorsement.

0.note: No school-choice statement found (council has no school jurisdiction).
No evidence found; religion unknown.

2.note: Voted YES (unanimous council, June 2026) on the FY2027 budget with a 1.7-cent property-tax increase; on council for the FY2024 4-cent increase; supports affordable-housing bonds. Her 2024 'steady-state budgeting' language noted but the tax-increase votes are the record.

0.note: Not a municipal function in NC; no statement found.

Does not support defunding, but no affirmative pro-police advocacy found — platform emphasizes shifting non-violent calls to unarmed responders, strengthening the Police Advisory Board, and 'use of excessive force is never acceptable'; endorsed by firefighters, not police. High-bar rule: routine budget votes don't count.
No evidence found (took office post-COVID).

2.note: No Patton-specific statement during the 2025-26 Raleigh ICE operations; coverage quotes Mayor Cowell, not Patton. No Raleigh sanctuary resolution found.

0.note: No constitutional-carry statement found.

1.note: Longtime Moms Demand Action member; per her own campaign bio she has 'called, emailed, and petitioned senators to enact reasonable gun legislation' — affirmative gun-control advocacy, contrary to opposing red-flag laws/magazine limits/AWBs.

profile.education: BA, Pratt Institute; graduate degree, Hunter College
profile.background: Urban planner (40+ years); Raleigh chief planning officer 2005-2014; NYC Parks Commissioner 2014-2021; past president, American Planning Association; principal at McAdams

mitchell-silver.notes_set: Raleigh City Council District A (nonpartisan; self-identified Democrat, age 64 at 2024 filing). Elected Nov 2024, unseating incumbent Mary Black — the council's most police-critical progressive — on an explicit pro-police-investment platform; running for re-election Nov 3 2026 (rematch vs Whitney Hill). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. DEVIATION FLAG: a Democrat with hard on-the-record pro-police advocacy ('increasing pay and benefits for law enforcement'; confidence in Chief Patterson). Fiscal record mixed: FY2026 no-tax-increase budget, then voted for the unanimous FY2027 1.7-cent property-tax increase after calling increases 'a last resort.' Sparse-data caveat: headline score rests on two cells; most culture-issue cells are null (no evidence found on abortion, marriage, schools, elections, immigration, guns; religion unknown).

0.note: No evidence found (questionnaires, campaign site, press all silent).
No statement found.

4.note: City Pride proclamations issue from the mayor's office; no Silver-specific statement, vote, or LGBT-PAC endorsement found.

0.note: No evidence found.
No evidence found; religion unknown (a campaign church-visit photo is not a position).

2.note: Voted YES (unanimous council, June 2026) on the FY2027 $1.77B budget with a 1.7-cent property-tax increase. Mitigating context noted: he publicly called a tax increase 'a last resort' (April 2026) and the FY2026 budget he supported held the rate flat.

0.note: No evidence found.

Affirmative on-the-record police backing: 'I am confident in the current leadership team led by Police Chief Estella Patterson' and campaign platform of 'increasing pay and benefits for law enforcement and first responders' (Oct 2024); unseated the council's police-critical incumbent on this platform. Clears the high bar — own advocacy, not just budget votes.
Not in office during COVID; no evidence found.

2.note: No Silver-specific statement during the Nov 2025 Raleigh ICE operations; no sanctuary measure located.

1.note: No ordinance, buy-back, or red-flag advocacy found.

set.office: City Council Member, At-Large (Mayor Pro Tem)

profile.education: BA sociology and MS rehabilitation counseling, UNC-Chapel Hill; JD, North Carolina Central University (2002)
profile.background: Attorney (general counsel/HR director, Southeastern Healthcare of NC); radio host; appointed to council July 2020 — first Black woman on Raleigh City Council; elected at-large 2022, re-elected 2024; Mayor Pro Tem

stormie-forte.notes_set: Raleigh City Council At-Large and Mayor Pro Tem (nonpartisan; registered Democrat). Appointed July 2020 (first Black woman on council; also first openly LGBTQ woman on council), elected at-large 2022, top at-large vote-getter 2024. Placed first (32.7%) in the March 3 2026 at-large primary; advances to the Nov 2026 general (two seats). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10: openly LGBTQ with sustained LGBT-advocacy endorsements (LGBTQ+ Victory Fund Feb 2026, Equality NC PAC, LPAC); consistent on-the-record pro-police advocacy across 2022/2024/2026 questionnaires (ACORNS 'a vital part of RPD'; more police/fire personnel a top 2026 issue; declined to endorse police-budget cuts); voted for the unanimous FY2027 tax-increase budget and supports a ~$100M affordable-housing bond. Her lone NO on the FY2022 budget came amid activist outcry over increased police funding, but her motive is undocumented — not scored. No evidence found on abortion (LPAC endorsement is soft), schools, religion, elections, or guns.

0.note: No direct statement or vote found; LPAC endorsement (reproductive-rights screening org) is soft — not scored.

First openly LGBTQ woman on Raleigh council; accepted the Feb 2026 LGBTQ+ Victory Fund endorsement ('has continued to stand out for LGBTQ+ equality') — contrary to affirming marriage as exclusively one man/one woman.

2.note: No trans-policy-specific statement or vote found in her name.

4.note: Accepted sustained single-issue LGBT-advocacy endorsements: LGBTQ+ Victory Fund (2026), Equality NC PAC (at-large), LPAC (2024).

0.note: No evidence found (council has no school jurisdiction; questionnaires silent).
No evidence found; religion unknown.

2.note: Voted YES (unanimous council, June 2026) on the FY2027 budget with a 1.7-cent property-tax increase; supports a ~$100M affordable-housing bond on the 2026 ballot (Feb 2026 questionnaire).

0.note: No evidence found.

Affirmative pro-police record across three cycles: 2026 — 'need for more police/fire personnel' a top issue, supports Chief Boyce's initiatives; 2024 — 'Chief Patterson has demonstrated sound leadership'; 2022 — called ACORNS 'a vital part of the Raleigh Police Department,' said added RPD staff/equipment would reduce stress in tense situations, and declined to endorse cutting the police budget. Clears the high bar.

1.note: No bail/crime-policy statement found. Her 2021 NO vote on a budget that increased police funding is unexplained in the record — not scored either direction.

0.note: No evidence found.

2.note: 2026 questionnaire: 'The City of Raleigh and RPD do not have jurisdiction for enforcing or participating in ICE operations... wants all residents and visitors to be safe and feel welcomed' — a jurisdictional/welcoming statement, not a sanctuary policy or a clear stance on the cell. Null.

0.note: No evidence found.

profile.education: BA, Princeton University; JD, UNC-Chapel Hill
profile.background: Mecklenburg County assistant DA 2007-2017; appointed DA Nov 2017 by Gov. Cooper — first Black DA of Mecklenburg County; raised in Mobile, AL
profile.prev_election_opponent: Tim Emry (2022 D primary, won 71-29; no GOP general opponent)

spencer-merriweather.notes_set: Mecklenburg County (Charlotte) DA since Nov 2017 (appointed; elected 2018, 2022). Both March 2026 primaries canceled; on the Nov 2026 general ballot, effectively unopposed (no GOP challenger identified). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. A mixed, not 'Soros-style,' Democrat: attacked from the LEFT in both elections; created a Violent Crimes Team and prioritized homicide/gun prosecutions; supports the death penalty per 2022 campaign coverage (soft source — not scored); requested an SBI investigation of Democratic 'sanctuary' Sheriff McFadden (Jan 2026, misconduct allegations). Contrary record also documented: first NC DA to call for ending cash bail (paired with preventive detention); 2020-21 declination policy on most drug-possession cases including some felonies; June 2022 pledge not to enforce abortion criminalization ('I don't see what is gained by putting a doctor or a woman on the stand'); accepted Equality NC and MeckPAC endorsements. Office faced a 2022 controversy when ~16,000 charges were erroneously dismissed during a COVID-backlog purge. Member, Prosecutors Against Gun Violence (not scored — coalition membership, no stated position on gun-law cells). Catholic (catechist at St. Peter, Charlotte).

0.note: No personhood statement found either way.

1.note: June 2022 (post-Dobbs): pledged his office would not enforce abortion criminalization — 'I don't see what is gained by putting a doctor or a woman on the stand... or prosecuting them for it.' Documented contrary position.

0.note: No direct marriage statement found.

4.note: Accepted endorsements from Equality NC and MeckPAC (LGBT-advocacy PACs), listed on his own campaign endorsements page.

0.note: No evidence found.
Practicing Catholic and parish catechist (profile fact), but no policy statement on public profession of faith — null.

2.note: Advocacy for more state prosecutor funding is an operational ask, not a deficit-spending position — null.

0.note: No evidence found.
No affirmative pro-police advocacy found; attacked from the left on police accountability, but that is others' framing, not his own advocacy — null per the high-bar rule.

1.note: First NC DA to call for ending cash bail ('there shouldn't be an amount of money that's an impediment'), and adopted a 2020-21 declination policy on most drug-possession cases including some felonies ('I do not have time to carry those cases') — documented cashless-bail and non-prosecution policies. Context noted: he paired the bail call with preventive detention of dangerous defendants.

2.note: Created a Violent Crimes Team with special gun/sexual-assault/DV units and publicly shifted office resources to homicide prosecution after a 2019 Observer probe showed weapons charges being dismissed — documented proportional-punishment posture toward violent offenders.

3.note: Mixed: his office labels violent crime 'a public health crisis,' yet simultaneously ramped up violent-crime prosecution rather than using the framing to avoid punishment. No clear alignment either way — null.

4.note: Death-penalty support reported only via his 2022 opponent's contrast (third-party characterization, soft) — held to null per the high-bar rule.

0.note: No evidence found; engaged constructively with the state's Iryna's Law rather than clashing.

2.note: Jan 2026: requested an SBI investigation of 'sanctuary' Sheriff McFadden — but over corruption/neglect-of-duty allegations, not sanctuary policy itself. No direct 287(g)/sanctuary stance found — null.

0.note: No evidence found.

1.note: Gun-crime prosecution priority and Prosecutors Against Gun Violence membership are not citable positions on red-flag laws/AWBs/registries — null.

profile.education: BA and JD, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
profile.background: Wake County Clerk of Superior Court 2006-2014; former NC Attorney General's office; first woman Wake County DA (elected 2014)

lorrin-freeman.notes_set: Sitting Wake County DA (elected 2014, 2018, 2022); announced May 8 2025 she will NOT seek re-election — retiring when her term ends Dec 2026 ('time to give someone else a chance'). Wiley Nickel won the March 3 2026 Democratic primary (49.0%) and is unopposed in November. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. DEVIATION FLAG — a Democrat DA with a hard traditional-prosecutor record: sought the death penalty at trial more than any NC county (first Wake death sentence in a decade, 2019); fast-tracks gun cases and backed the RPD-DOJ violent-crime partnership; the only Triangle DA to DECLINE the post-Dobbs non-prosecution pledge; prosecuted the McCrae Dowless absentee-ballot-fraud scheme AND criminally investigated Democratic AG Josh Stein; criticized the Wake sheriff's ICE non-cooperation ('there needs to be some middle ground'). Attacked consistently from the LEFT ('pretend progressive'; INDY endorsed her 2022 primary opponent). Mixed/null items: chaired an incremental, safety-conditioned pretrial-reform study (not cashless bail — null); criticized GOP budget language targeting DA discretion; urged ICE off courthouses. Sparse-data caveat: headline score rests on the public-justice/immigration cells where her record is strong; all culture-issue cells are null (no evidence found on marriage, schools, religion).

0.note: No personhood statement found.

1.note: Notable deviation but not scoreable: the only Triangle DA to DECLINE the May 2022 pledge not to prosecute abortion (citing her position as a defendant in a Planned Parenthood suit), while noting her office has never prosecuted under existing abortion laws. Neither abolition advocacy nor a contrary pro-abortion position — null.

0.note: No evidence found.

4.note: No Pride participation, statements, or LGBT-PAC endorsements found.

0.note: No evidence found.
No evidence found; religion unknown.

2.note: No fiscal-policy positions found (capital-trial cost criticism is third-party).

0.note: No stance found on ballot/machine method. Context for the record: her office prosecuted the McCrae Dowless absentee-ballot-fraud scheme (2016/2018 elections; four co-conspirators pleaded guilty 2022) and criminally investigated Democratic AG Josh Stein under the false-campaign-statement law — a strong election-law-enforcement record, including against her own party, but not a position on these specific cells.

4.note: No stance found on private election funding or foreign interference.

0.note: Publicly backed the RPD-U.S. DOJ violent-crime-reduction partnership; widely characterized as a traditional prosecutor allied with law enforcement (declined charges in officer-involved shootings); no defund sympathy anywhere in the record.

1.note: Mixed: chaired Wake's pretrial-reform study (expanded release, risk-assessment tool) — but explicitly incremental and only 'without jeopardizing public safety,' not cashless bail; simultaneously attacked from the left as a 'pretend progressive.' No clear alignment either way — null.

2.note: Documented proportional-punishment record: Wake under Freeman sought the death penalty at trial more than any NC county (Gillard, 2019 — first Wake death sentence in over a decade); fast-tracks gun cases to indictment.

3.note: No public-health-framing statements found either way.

4.note: Actively pursued capital punishment in office; her own words (2022): the death penalty 'should be reserved for only those most egregious murder cases, and only as long as it continues to be North Carolina law' — affirms the state's capital sword in practice and statement.

0.note: She criticized GOP budget language targeting DA discretion as a 'chilling effect' — a prosecutorial-discretion dispute, not an unconstitutional-mandate assertion in the rubric's sense — null.

2.note: Publicly criticized the Wake sheriff's ICE non-cooperation after the release of an illegal-alien sex offender: 'there needs to be some middle ground... using all the tools available' to hold threats — documented opposition to sanctuary-style non-cooperation. Caveat noted: she separately urged ICE to avoid courthouse enforcement as counter-productive.

0.note: No evidence found.

1.note: Gun-case prosecution priority is not a position on gun-law cells — null.
